library_preparation_preflight.summary: Apache Pulsar Java client is primarily exercised through producer/consumer/reader operations against a running Pulsar broker; the artifact is otherwise self-contained, but meaningful end-to-end coverage needs a standalone broker.

deterministic_setup.reason: Needed by generated JUnit tests to start and manage a disposable standalone Pulsar broker for producer/consumer coverage.
Official Pulsar image matching the requested client version; documentation uses standalone Pulsar on ports 6650 and 8080 for client examples.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Start a standalone broker with command `bin/pulsar standalone`, expose broker port `6650` and HTTP/admin port `8080`, wait for readiness before creating the client, and build clients with `PulsarClient.builder().serviceUrl("pulsar://<host>:<mapped-6650>").build()`. Use unique topic names under `persistent://public/default/` and close producers, consumers, readers, and clients to avoid thread/resource leaks.
risks: The standalone Pulsar container can be slow to start and may be resource-heavy in native-image test runs.

Without the broker setup, tests can still cover builders, schemas, authentication factories, and message-id utilities, but they will miss the core producer/consumer path that defines normal client usage.
